text:
# Data Controls and Reconciliation implementation  
IBM Confidential  
4  
FCTP DR Assessment Report  | 9th February 2024  
4  
4  
EDB (CAO/SAO)  
Country of birth  
Webservice (PUT)  
SAMM  
countryOfBirth  
Webservice (PUT)  
PLPOS  
CountryOfBirthCode  
Applicant table  
Summit(TMW)  
Country of birth  
Cust\_personal\_details  
QL  
BirthCountryCode, BirthCountryCodeJoint1  
QLMLRDATA  
QH  
BirthCountryCode  
ITISEARCH

CISDBT  
Table : BA001  
Column : COUBIRISO  
Table : CIS\_CISODS\_BA001\_SRI

CIS  
| IMAS (MLR)                                            |    | NeRDE  (inbound file)                                                         |    | NeRDE (Feed Schema)                         |    | NeRDE  (transform schema)                      |    | NeRDE(IFS)                                                  |    | Net Reveal  Database                          |
|-------------------------------------------------------|----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----|---------------------------------------------|----|------------------------------------------------|----|-------------------------------------------------------------|----|-----------------------------------------------|
| Table :  MLR_MCDD_HIST_ACC  Column : BIRTH_COUNTRY_CD |    | File: IMAS_NeRDE_M_CDD_YYYYMMDD_YYYYMMDDHHMISS.txt   Field : BIRTH_COUNTRY_CD |    | Table: MLR_MCDD_Raw  Column: CountryOfBirth |    | Table :  Customers  Column : COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN |    | Table : CUSTOMERS_YYYYMMDD.txt  Column :  COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN |    | Table :  CUSTOMERS  Column: COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN |
| Column : BIRTH_COUNTRY_CD                             |    | BIRTH_COUNTRY_CD                                                              |    | CountryOfBirth                              |    | COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN                              |    | COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN                                           |    | COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN                             |  
Business Control  (Sample)  
The ‘Country of Origin’  data element must be maintained in its original state  from the point of entry  into the system  through to  its utilization in  the risk rating system  within the CDD system. Any modification, alteration , or update  to this data element is  strictly prohibited  across all intermediary  systems and stages  of data processing.  
FCTP CDE “Country Of Origin” as derived from CDD (Customer Due Diligence) output  
Data Reconciliation Controls and Checks  
CDE Level : Customer data transmission is deemed successfully completed only when  
1. The number of customer records with ‘Country of  Origin’ matches between source and target
2. For every customer record the ‘Country of Origin’ field matches between source and target (String / checksum matching )
3. For every customer record , the format and data type of  ‘Country of origin’ field has not undergone any changes (like ‘Germany’ to  ‘Deutschland’ OR Country name to ISO or vice versa)
4. Invalid  ‘Country of  Origin’  should be flagged and  target system should be informed about those  
Interface Level  Key Controls (applicable for all CDEs):  
1. Every interface need to have job control (system audit, error threshold , altering & restart process)  implemented (either as in-built construct or post job process)
2. In the event of  any data or count mismatch (exceeding the threshold), the interface  must halt data loading and trigger a alert.
3. A detailed log should be maintained for every loading  operation with issues separately highlighted for resolution.  
Legend  
Reconciliation Control Points
name: Chunk 5/5

raw_entities.name: FCTP DR Assessment Report
raw_entities.description: A formal data resilience and recovery assessment report dated 9th February 2024. It outlines the data controls, reconciliation processes, and system mappings required for the FCTP initiative. The document serves as a reference for validating data transmission and interface controls across multiple systems.

raw_entities.name: EDB
raw_entities.description: A primary database system referenced in the data flow. It handles the 'Country of birth' data element and interfaces with downstream systems via a Webservice (PUT) mechanism.

raw_entities.name: SAMM
raw_entities.description: A system component within the data pipeline that processes the 'countryOfBirth' field. It receives data through a Webservice (PUT) interface and passes it to subsequent processing stages.

raw_entities.name: PLPOS
raw_entities.description: A system responsible for managing the 'CountryOfBirthCode' data element. It acts as an intermediary node in the data transmission chain before final reconciliation.

raw_entities.name: Summit
raw_entities.description: A platform (TMW) referenced in the data flow that manages the 'Country of birth' data element. It serves as a processing node between upstream and downstream systems.

raw_entities.name: CIS
raw_entities.description: A Customer Information System functioning as an Operational Data Store (ODS). It contains specific tables and columns, such as BA001 and COUBIRISO, used for storing and querying customer data elements.

raw_entities.name: IMAS
raw_entities.description: A system (MLR) containing historical account tables and columns used for tracking customer birth country data. It feeds into the broader reconciliation and control framework.

raw_entities.name: NeRDE
raw_entities.description: A comprehensive data processing framework encompassing inbound files, feed schemas, transform schemas, and IFS components. It handles data ingestion, transformation, and schema management for downstream consumption.

raw_entities.name: Net Reveal Database
raw_entities.description: A database system referenced in the data flow that stores and manages the 'COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN' field. It serves as a target or intermediate repository for customer origin data.

raw_entities.name: Data Reconciliation Controls and Checks
raw_entities.description: A structured validation framework ensuring data transmission success across source and target systems. It mandates record count matching, string/checksum field verification, and strict preservation of data formats and types. Invalid data is automatically flagged to trigger corrective actions.
context: The number of customer records with ‘Country of  Origin’ matches between source and target
the format and data type of   ‘Country of origin’ field has not undergone any changes
Invalid   ‘Country of  Origin’  should be flagged and  target system should be informed about those

raw_entities.name: Interface Level Key Controls
raw_entities.description: A set of operational safeguards applied to all data elements across system interfaces. It requires built-in job controls, automated halt triggers for threshold-exceeding mismatches, and comprehensive logging for issue resolution and audit trails.
context: Interface Level  Key Controls (applicable for all CDEs)
the interface  must halt data loading and trigger a alert
A detailed log should be maintained for every loading  operation with issues separately highlighted for resolution

raw_entities.name: Country of Origin Data Maintenance
raw_entities.description: A critical data element (CDE) control requiring strict state preservation from initial system entry through final utilization. The framework explicitly prohibits any modification, alteration, or update to this data element across all intermediary systems and processing stages.
context: The ‘Country of Origin’  data element must be maintained in its original state
Any modification, alteration , or update  to this data element is  strictly prohibited
across all intermediary  systems and stages  of data processing

raw_entities.name: Customer Due Diligence (CDD)
raw_entities.description: A regulatory and risk management process that serves as the authoritative source for deriving the 'Country Of Origin' data element. The CDD system outputs this data for downstream utilization in risk rating and reconciliation workflows.
context: FCTP CDE “Country Of Origin” as derived from CDD (Customer Due Diligence) output
utilization in  the risk rating system  within the CDD system

raw_entities.name: FCTP
raw_entities.description: The primary organizational or project entity responsible for the data controls, reconciliation framework, and DR assessment report. It governs the end-to-end data transmission standards and compliance requirements outlined in the document.

raw_entities.name: Business Control Framework
raw_entities.description: A sample control framework governing data element maintenance and state preservation. It establishes strict rules to prevent unauthorized modifications, ensuring data integrity from system entry through to risk rating utilization.
context: The ‘Country of Origin’  data element must be maintained in its original state

context: The ‘Country of Origin’  data element must be maintained in its original state

key_characteristics: Functions as CAO/SAO (Country of Origin/Source of Authority)
Stores Country of birth in Cust_personal_details
Stores COUBIRISO column in BA001 and CIS_CISODS_BA001_SRI tables
Functions as MLR (Money Laundering Reporting) system
Handles inbound files and feed/transform schemas

properties.what_it_does: Validates customer data transmission integrity at the CDE level
technical_components: Record count matching between source and target
String/checksum matching for every customer record
Invalid data flagging and target system alerting
properties.differentiating_aspect: Ensures strict data fidelity and prevents unauthorized modifications across transmission

properties.what_it_does: Enforces operational controls and error handling at the interface level
technical_components: Job control (system audit, error threshold, altering & restart process)
Automatic halt on data/count mismatch exceeding threshold
Detailed logging with issue highlighting for resolution
properties.differentiating_aspect: Prevents data corruption propagation by halting loads and enforcing strict audit trails

properties.what_it_does: Ensures the ‘Country of Origin’ data element remains unaltered from entry to utilization
technical_components: End-to-end state preservation across intermediary systems
properties.differentiating_aspect: Enforces zero-tolerance policy for data alteration to maintain compliance and risk rating accuracy

properties.problem_context: Deriving and maintaining accurate Country of Origin data for risk rating and compliance
properties.approach: Extracting CDE from CDD output and enforcing strict data controls across systems
properties.outcome_achieved: Accurate risk rating and regulatory compliance

properties.scope: Data controls and reconciliation for CDEs across systems
properties.target_client_profile: financial institutions requiring strict data integrity and compliance

relationships.description: The assessment report involves the FCTP organization as the primary subject and beneficiary.
The business control framework comprises data reconciliation controls and checks to ensure data integrity.
The business control framework comprises interface level key controls for system audit and error handling.
relationships.context: Interface Level Key Controls (applicable for all CDEs):

relationships.description: The business control framework comprises country of origin data maintenance to prevent unauthorized modifications.
relationships.context: The ‘Country of Origin’ data element must be maintained in its original state from the point of entry into the system through to its utilization in the risk rating system within the CDD system.

relationships.description: Maintaining accurate country of origin data enables effective customer due diligence processes.
relationships.context: FCTP CDE “Country Of Origin” as derived from CDD (Customer Due Diligence) output

relationships.description: The business control framework is applicable to the customer due diligence system environment.
The business control framework is demonstrated and documented at the FCTP DR Assessment Report.
The business control framework is targeted for the FCTP organization to govern data controls.
Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the EDB technology at the source system level.
Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the SAMM technology during webservice transmission.
Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the PLPOS technology for applicant data processing.
Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the Summit technology for customer personal details.
Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the CIS technology at the ODS/database level.
relationships.context: CIS (ODS) ... CIS (Database) ... Table : BA001 ... Column : COUBIRISO

relationships.description: Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the IMAS technology for MLR historical data.
Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the NeRDE technology across inbound, feed, and transform schemas.
relationships.context: NeRDE (inbound file) ... NeRDE (Feed Schema) ... NeRDE(transform schema) ... NeRDE(IFS)

relationships.description: Data reconciliation controls are delivered via the Net Reveal Database at the final target storage stage.
relationships.context: Net Reveal Database ... Table : CUSTOMERS Column: COUNTRY_OF_ORIGIN

relationships.description: EDB feeds into SAMM via webservice for country of birth data transmission.
SAMM feeds into PLPOS via webservice for country of birth code transmission.
PLPOS feeds into Summit for applicant table data processing.
Summit feeds into CIS for customer personal details and ODS data storage.
CIS feeds into IMAS for MLR historical data processing.
IMAS feeds into NeRDE via inbound file for CDD data extraction.
NeRDE feeds into Net Reveal Database via transform/IFS for final customer data storage.
Interface level key controls are delivered via the EDB technology interface for job control and error handling.
relationships.context: Interface Level Key Controls (applicable for all CDEs): 1. Every interface need to have job control (system audit, error threshold , altering & restart process) implemented

relationships.description: Interface level key controls are delivered via the Net Reveal Database interface for data mismatch alerting and logging.
relationships.context: Interface Level Key Controls (applicable for all CDEs): 2. In the event of any data or count mismatch ... the interface must halt data loading and trigger a alert. 3. A detailed log should be maintained for every loading operation
